title: Hunting for the Best Beef Noodles in Taipei

all.en: When my cousin Daniel flew in from Vancouver, his first request was simple but serious.
all.zh: 當我表哥丹尼爾從溫哥華飛來時，他的第一個要求簡單卻認真。

all.en: He wanted to find the absolute best bowl of beef noodles in all of Taipei.
all.zh: 他想找到整個台北最頂級的一碗牛肉麵。

all.en: I laughed at first, thinking he was joking, but his face was completely serious.
all.zh: 起初我笑了，以為他在開玩笑，但他的表情卻完全嚴肅。

all.en: He had been planning his beef noodle map for almost six months online.
all.zh: 他在網路上規劃他的牛肉麵地圖已經將近六個月了。

all.en: He had a list of fifteen famous shops, sorted by neighborhood and broth style.
all.zh: 他列了一張十五家名店的清單，依社區和湯頭風格分類。

all.en: On the first morning, we started at a small shop near Yongkang Street.
all.zh: 第一天早上，我們從永康街附近的一家小店開始。

all.en: The broth was deep brown, rich with soy sauce, star anise, and a hint of orange peel.
all.zh: 湯頭呈深棕色，醬油、八角和一絲橙皮香氣濃郁。

all.en: Daniel closed his eyes after the first sip and whispered that it was incredible.
all.zh: 丹尼爾喝下第一口後閉上眼睛，輕聲說這真是太不可思議了。

all.en: By lunchtime, we had walked thirty minutes to try a famous shop near the main station.
all.zh: 到了午餐時間，我們走了三十分鐘到車站附近的一家名店。

all.en: Their version used clear broth with tender beef shank and thin handmade noodles.
all.zh: 他們的版本是清燉湯頭，配上軟嫩的牛腱肉和細手工麵條。

all.en: Daniel took careful notes on his phone, rating each bowl on flavor, texture, and balance.
all.zh: 丹尼爾在手機上仔細做筆記，從味道、口感和平衡度為每碗麵打分。

all.en: I started to wonder if my stomach could survive his ambitious eating schedule.
all.zh: 我開始懷疑我的胃是否承受得住他這雄心勃勃的進食行程。

all.en: On day two, we took the MRT to a working-class neighborhood far from the tourists.
all.zh: 第二天，我們搭捷運到一個遠離觀光客的勞工社區。

all.en: The shop had no English menu and only six small tables crammed against the walls.
all.zh: 那家店沒有英文菜單，沿牆只擠著六張小桌子。

all.en: The owner, a tiny woman in her seventies, ladled broth like she had done it for decades.
all.zh: 老闆是位七十多歲的嬌小婦人，舀湯的動作彷彿已經做了數十年。

all.en: Her tomato beef noodles were tangy, savory, and unlike anything Daniel had tasted before.
all.zh: 她的番茄牛肉麵酸甜鹹香，是丹尼爾以前從未嘗過的味道。

all.en: He immediately moved her shop to the top of his list, just above the Yongkang spot.
all.zh: 他立刻把這家店移到清單第一位，就排在永康街那家之上。

all.en: On the third day, we ate at three different shops within a single afternoon.
all.zh: 第三天，我們在一個下午內連續吃了三家不同的店。

all.en: My stomach groaned, but Daniel only smiled and ordered another small bowl.
all.zh: 我的胃在抗議，但丹尼爾只是微笑著又點了一碗小碗。

all.en: We chatted with one owner who shared the history of his family recipe from Sichuan.
all.zh: 我們和一位老闆聊天，他分享了家族食譜源自四川的歷史。

all.en: He told us that the secret was patience, not spices or expensive cuts of meat.
all.zh: 他告訴我們，秘訣是耐心，而不是香料或昂貴的肉品部位。

all.en: Daniel wrote that quote into his notebook and underlined it twice for emphasis.
all.zh: 丹尼爾把這句話寫進筆記本，並劃了兩次線以示重視。

all.en: By the end of the week, we had visited twelve shops and skipped only three.
all.zh: 到了週末，我們已經拜訪了十二家店，只略過了三家。

all.en: Daniel's clothes felt tighter, and his shoes had walked through half the city.
all.zh: 丹尼爾的衣服變緊了，他的鞋子也走遍了半個城市。

all.en: On the final evening, he asked me to choose the winning bowl for him.
all.zh: 在最後一個晚上，他請我替他選出冠軍的那一碗。

all.en: I felt the weight of his trust, knowing how seriously he had taken this trip.
all.zh: 我感受到他這份信任的份量，深知他對這趟旅程有多麼認真。

all.en: We returned to the tiny shop run by the elderly woman in the quiet neighborhood.
all.zh: 我們回到那位住在安靜社區、年長婦人經營的小店。

all.en: She remembered Daniel and added an extra slice of beef without being asked.
all.zh: 她記得丹尼爾，沒等他開口就多加了一片牛肉。

all.en: Daniel took photos of every detail, from the chopsticks to the chipped wooden counter.
all.zh: 丹尼爾拍下了每一個細節，從筷子到那磨損的木製吧台。

all.en: He declared her shop the official winner of his entire Taipei beef noodle adventure.
all.zh: 他宣布她的店是他整個台北牛肉麵冒險的官方冠軍。

all.en: The owner blushed and waved her hand modestly, saying we were exaggerating too much.
all.zh: 老闆害羞地揮揮手，謙虛地說我們太誇張了。

all.en: She gave us each a small bag of dried noodles as a parting gift for the road.
all.zh: 她送給我們每人一小袋乾麵作為臨別禮物。

all.en: On the way to the airport, Daniel said this trip was the happiest week of his year.
all.zh: 在前往機場的路上，丹尼爾說這趟旅程是他這一年最快樂的一週。

all.en: He promised he would write a long blog post celebrating every shop we visited.
all.zh: 他承諾會寫一篇長長的部落格文章，介紹我們造訪過的每一家店。

all.en: I waved goodbye at the gate, already planning our next noodle hunt for his return.
all.zh: 我在登機門揮手道別，心裡已經開始規劃他下次回來時的麵食探訪行程了。
